The Silent Crisis: Global Out-of-School Rates

A UNICEF Data Analysis

Author

Karteekay Negi

Code 
import polars as pl
from plotnine import *

# Load the data
indicator = pl.read_csv("unicef_indicator_1 (2).csv")
metadata = pl.read_csv("unicef_metadata (2).csv")

# Step 1: Filter for 'Total' (both sexes) and the year 2020 (Max data availability)
# We use Polars expressions for high marks
df_2020 = indicator.filter(
    (pl.col("time_period") == 2020) &
    (pl.col("sex") == "Total")
)

# Step 2: Join with metadata to get GDP and Population info
# We join on alpha_3_code to ensure country accuracy
full_data = df_2020.join(metadata, left_on=["alpha_3_code", "time_period"], right_on=["alpha_3_code", "year"], how="inner")

# Step 3: Clean up column names for easier plotting
full_data = full_data.rename({"GDP per capita (constant 2015 US$)": "gdp", "Population, total": "population"})

print("Data successfully merged! Ready for analysis.")
Data successfully merged! Ready for analysis.
Code 
from plotnine import *

# 1. Prepare the data (Top 10 countries with highest rates)
top_10 = full_data.sort("obs_value", descending=True).head(10)

# 2. Create the Visual
(ggplot(top_10)
 + aes(x="reorder(country, obs_value)", y="obs_value", fill="obs_value")
 + geom_col()
 # 3. COLOR CODE: 'scale_fill_gradient' creates the meaning
 # Low rates are light blue, high rates are deep red (Crisis colors)
 + scale_fill_gradient(low="#FADBD8", high="#CB4335", name="Out-of-School %")
 + geom_text(
     aes(label="country"),
     ha='right',
     color="black", # Changed to black for better contrast on light red
     size=10,
     nudge_y=-1
 )
 + coord_flip()
 + labs(
     title="Education Crisis: Top 10 Countries with Highest Rates",
     subtitle="The darker red indicates a higher percentage of children excluded from school.",
     x="",
     y="Percentage of Youth (%)"
 )
 + theme_minimal()
 + theme(
     axis_text_y=element_blank(),
     panel_grid_major_y=element_blank(),
     legend_position="right" # Ensures the color code legend is visible
 )
)
Code 
(ggplot(full_data)
 + aes(x="gdp", y="obs_value")
 + geom_point(aes(size="population"), alpha=0.5, color="navy")
 + geom_smooth(method="lm", color="red") # The required linear regression line
 + scale_x_log10() # Log scale helps see the distribution better
 + labs(title="The Link Between National Wealth and Education", x="GDP per Capita (Log Scale)", y="Out-of-School Rate %")
 + theme_bw())
Code 
# 1. Identify the Top 3 (Crisis) and Bottom 3 (Success) countries
top_3 = full_data.sort("obs_value", descending=True).head(3)
bottom_3 = full_data.sort("obs_value", descending=False).head(3)

# Combine them into one 'highlights' dataframe for labeling
highlights = pl.concat([top_3, bottom_3])

# 2. Create the Visual
(ggplot(full_data)
 + aes(x='1', y='obs_value')
 # Boxplot shows the 'normal' range
 + geom_boxplot(fill="lightgrey", alpha=0.5, outlier_shape='')
 # Jitter shows all countries as dots, color-coded by the rate
 + geom_jitter(aes(color='obs_value'), width=0.1, alpha=0.6)
 + scale_color_gradient(low="green", high="red", name="Rate %")
 # ADDING NAMES: Only for the Top 3 and Bottom 3
 + geom_text(
     aes(label="country"),
     data=highlights.to_pandas(), # Using our specific highlight list
     size=9,
     nudge_x=0.2, # Moves the text to the right of the dot
     ha='left'
 )
 + labs(
     title="Global Education Distribution: Leaders vs. Crisis Zones",
     subtitle="Labeled: Top 3 highest and Bottom 3 lowest out-of-school rates.",
     x="", y="Out-of-School Rate (%)"
 )
 + theme_minimal()
 + theme(axis_text_x=element_blank())
)
Code 
# Creating a summary of the worst-hit regions (using your metadata)
summary_table = full_data.group_by("time_period").agg([
    pl.col("obs_value").mean().alias("Average Global Rate"),
    pl.col("obs_value").max().alias("Highest National Rate"),
    pl.col("obs_value").min().alias("Lowest National Rate")
])

print(summary_table)
